parsed_review.summary: CP00-936 is a clean descriptor-only EmployeeFixture/CandidateFixture HRX bridge pack (RP30.P05.M01.S08-RP30.P05.M02.S08, risk A, 10 units) and its modest claim (production_ready=false, runtime_ready=false, descriptor_only) is fully supported. The HRX_CP936_PACK_BINDING, required-section rows, and requirements (registry.js) match the live closeout-pack-plan entry exactly with deviation_from_plan=false. The descriptor factory and generated domain rows hold every runtime/record-write/real-data/permission-bypass/identity-link flag at false, spread HRX_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION and HRX_BLOCKED_CLAIMS, and the validators (validators.js) plus three CP936 tests (model.test.js) enforce and assert these boundaries, with the regenerated contract pinned to CP00-936 as latest projection. Authority boundary is correctly maintained: Claude is read-only and not promoted to final approval, human approval is required for any runtime opening (deferred to CP00-937), and the Hermes packet is deterministic review-packet metadata (emits_runtime_receipt=false), not embedded product runtime. Manifest, construction-inspection, adjudication, command-evidence, and claude-review-result are consistently staged pending-review with no real data, no writes, and no invalid review evidence passed off as valid (readiness auth-preflight passed with non-empty valid JSON). Changed-file scope is respected; unrelated dirty/untracked files are segregated and not attributed to this pack. blocks_goal_closeout=false because the pack claims neither runtime nor enterprise trust, and the expected human/enterprise-trust approval authority boundary lies outside this receipt. Provenance note: this verdict rests on direct read-only source inspection (Read/Grep/Glob) as the packet mandates; the full npm validation ladder is orchestrator-run and recorded as command evidence, not re-executed in this review. No P0/P1/P2/P3 findings.
